strategy_canvas.headline_thesis: Market Tracker bridges three previously-unbridged things — branded academic methodology (Booth EMBA modules), AI-narrated rendering on live financials, and a SE-Asia distribution channel via Elitez Group — that no SG retail-research player owns end-to-end.

segments.name: Booth/EMBA Practitioner
segments.descriptor: EMBA / MBA candidate / alumni using CF/FS/OM/CS frameworks

segments.descriptor: 30-50 yr-old retail with > S$50k portfolio; reads earnings

segments.name: Elitez SME CFO
segments.descriptor: CFO / Finance Director inside Elitez Group SG SME client base

attack_plans.icp: Booth EMBA candidate / alumni using CF / FS / OM / CS frameworks for both classroom case-studies and personal-investment idea generation; benchmarks against Damodaran XLS + ChatGPT today
attack_plans.tam_reasoning: EMBA + active alumni ~250k globally; Booth-aligned cohort (Booth + adjacent programmes) ~80k; assume 10% conversion at SGD 39/mo Pro = SGD 3.7M ARR. EMBA-cohort site licences ~30 programmes adopting at SGD 4,500/yr = SGD 135k. Plus alumni Pro halo. Total addressable ~SGD 6.5M.
attack_plans.why_gap: Damodaran XLS is rigorous but manual + Excel-only. ChatGPT is fluent but ungrounded in framework. Simply Wall St is fluent but proprietary framework, not academic. No SE-Asia retail tool surfaces a *brand-named* academic framework rendered live on any ticker.
attack_plans.why_we_win: Market Tracker's four modules are explicit Booth-EMBA framework digitisation. Tooling + narrative + reproducibility + share-as-PDF in one app; persona doesn't trade off rigour for speed any more.

gtm.channel: EMBA classroom invites (Booth Singapore, NUS, SMU); alumni Slack/LinkedIn; SHRI / SMU EMBA mailing lists; Booth alumni hub guest posts
gtm.pitch: Damodaran rigour, ChatGPT-fluency, run on any S&P 500 ticker in 30 seconds. Built around CF / FS / OM / CS modules, citation-grade. Free guest tier; Pro at S$29; cohort site licence S$4,500/yr.
gtm.pricing: Pro consumer SGD 29/mo; EMBA-cohort site licence SGD 4,500/yr
gtm.content: Booth methodology page; case-study templates; comparison vs Damodaran XLS; alumni hub partnership

attack_plans.niche_name: Boutique Fund 'CFO of the Stock' Memo
attack_plans.icp: Junior-mid analyst at SG/HK family office or boutique fund (3-15 staff, AUM S$50M-S$500M); shared Bloomberg + Capital IQ seat; PM expects framework rigour in memos
attack_plans.tam_reasoning: SG + HK boutique funds + family offices ~750 firms; assume 30% adopt Team tier (3 seats avg) at SGD 199/mo = SGD 5.4M annualised. Conservative because procurement friction is real for institutional buyers.
attack_plans.why_gap: Bloomberg is too expensive per analyst; Capital IQ shared seats create queue waits; TIKR has data depth but no framework lens; AlphaSense is search not framework. No tool turns 6 hrs of CF + FS + OM + CS memo-compile into a 30-sec auto-generated PDF.
attack_plans.why_we_win: Time-saving math: junior analyst at S$80/hr loaded x 4 hrs saved x 8 names/mo = S$2,560/mo opportunity-cost vs SGD 199/mo Team tier. Plus framework rigour is exactly what PM wants in memos. Plus the team can collaborate.

gtm.channel: Direct outreach to SG/HK boutique fund founder-PMs via SHRI / IMAS / HKVCA networks; LinkedIn warm intros via SG family-office analyst community
gtm.pitch: Save your junior 30 hrs/mo of memo compile. SGD 199/mo for 3 seats. Booth-grade framework rigour, citable, PM-readable, exportable. Saves 8% of your analyst cost, full stop.
gtm.pricing: Team tier SGD 199/mo (3 seats); per-seat add-on SGD 50/mo
gtm.content: Boutique-fund case study; 6-hrs-to-30-sec memo time-saving deck; Booth memo template open-source

attack_plans.icp: 30-50 yr-old SG / MY / HK self-directed retail investor with S$50-500k portfolio; outgrew moomoo / Tiger AI cards; reads earnings; benchmarks Simply Wall St as the alt
attack_plans.tam_reasoning: SE-Asia prosumer pool ~1.1M; assume 4% reach + 25% conversion at SGD 25/mo Pro effective ARPU = SGD 3.3M annualised. Plus broker-partner co-distribution lift (moomoo / Tiger SG content-partner deals) potentially adding SGD 8M-10M cumulative TAM. Modeled at SGD 12M.
attack_plans.why_gap: moomoo / Tiger broker AI cards are too shallow for conviction-building; Yahoo Finance has no framework lens; Bloomberg/FactSet are out of reach; Simply Wall St uses proprietary framework not academic. The lane between 'broker AI card' and 'pro-grade terminal' is wide open in SE-Asia at SGD 25-29/mo.
attack_plans.why_we_win: Booth-academic provenance + AI-narrative grounded in financial statements + SE-Asia distribution via Elitez channel + bilingual narrative (English-first, Chinese roadmap). Three things no SE-Asia rival owns.

gtm.channel: SG retail investor Slack/Discord; broker-app content partnership (moomoo SG content team, Tiger SG education hub); SGX investor-education content; YouTube creator partnerships (SG finance creators 50k+ subs)
gtm.pitch: When the broker-app summary card isn't enough but Bloomberg is out of reach. SGD 29/mo. Booth-EMBA framework on every name you watch.
gtm.pricing: Free guest tier; Pro consumer SGD 29/mo
gtm.content: Booth-vs-snowflake comparison content; 'after the broker app' explainer; SGX-listed-name framework gallery

attack_plans.icp: CFO / Finance Director at SG SME (50-300 headcount, S$10-100M revenue) inside the Elitez Group client base; warm relationship via Elitez Pulse; uses framework for personal portfolio + occasional board-pack capital-markets context
attack_plans.tam_reasoning: Elitez Pulse / EOR / Aviation client base ~2,000 SG SMEs; assume 1 finance leader per firm + 30% reach + 25% conversion at SGD 29/mo Pro personal = SGD 720k annualised. Volume-seeding tier, not GMV-engine.
attack_plans.why_gap: CFOs default to Yahoo Finance + ChatGPT today; resist firm-paid tooling; treat research as personal interest. No tool today bundles framework rigour + 1-page board-pack-grade PDF + Elitez-trusted distribution.
attack_plans.why_we_win: Warm Elitez Pulse channel zeroes CAC. CFO trusts the brand-stack and reads the PDF on the train. Volume-seeds the Pro tier and produces testimonial mass + EMBA referrals (SG SME CFOs often have an EMBA spouse / colleague).

gtm.channel: Elitez Pulse account-team cross-sell on monthly client-success review; Elitez SME CFO roundtables + lunches; SHRI HR-Finance joint events
gtm.pitch: Your morning-coffee read on any S&P 500 stock, Booth-framework-rigorous, PDF-shareable. SGD 29/mo personal. Elitez clients get first 60 days free.
gtm.pricing: Pro consumer SGD 29/mo (Elitez client 60-day free trial)
gtm.content: SME CFO morning-read sample PDFs; Elitez Pulse co-marketed launch deck; 'board pack one-pager' framework template

attack_plans.icp: Tech-savvy retail investor (ChatGPT Plus / Claude Pro user) who already pastes earnings transcripts into chat for narrative analysis; wants 'grounded ChatGPT' not 'another retail SaaS'
attack_plans.tam_reasoning: SE-Asia ChatGPT Plus / Claude Pro user base ~3M; ~5% use it for finance regularly; ~10% conversion to a paid framework-grounded tool at SGD 9-29/mo blended ARPU = SGD 1.8M annualised.
attack_plans.why_gap: ChatGPT is fluent but hallucinates equity data without source-grounding; re-prompting for the same framework template is friction; no persistence between conversations on a ticker. Persona explicitly resists 'retail SaaS' framing — wants ChatGPT-augmenter not ChatGPT-replacer.
attack_plans.why_we_win: Visible methodology lineage (model used: Claude Sonnet, retrieval scope: yfinance financial statements, prompt template public) builds the trust bridge from DIY-ChatGPT to grounded-framework. Free guest tier with rate limit converts at the 'I'd hit rate limit anyway with ChatGPT Plus' moment.

gtm.channel: ChatGPT GPT marketplace; Claude / Anthropic developer community; SE-Asia AI-curious retail subreddits + Hacker News
gtm.pitch: ChatGPT-fluency + grounded financial statements + Booth-EMBA framework. The hallucination-free way to talk to an AI about a stock. Free guest. Pro SGD 29/mo.
gtm.content: Methodology + AI-guardrails page; comparison vs ChatGPT-with-no-data; open prompt template + retrieval-scope disclosure
